commit.message:
Fix LinkedIn token check: URL-encode credentials in curl request

The client_secret contains special characters (+, =) that get mangled
in application/x-www-form-urlencoded when using curl -d. Switch to
--data-urlencode so curl properly encodes each parameter.

commit.message:
Add tmap composition vignette with style registry integration (#26)

* Add Neexdzii Kwa spatial data and document all datasets

Generate subbasin data (Johnny David to Richfield on Bulkley mainstem,
~212 km²) via fresh package. Document both Bittner Creek and Neexdzii Kwa
datasets with roxygen.

Phase 1 of gq#17.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add tmap composition vignette for Neexdzii Kwa subbasin

Full map composition using registry styles: basemap blend, watershed,
streams, lakes, wetlands, roads, railway, keymap inset. All colors
from gq_reg_main(). Drop Z/M from bundled data for GEOS compat.

Stream/road widths use manual multiplier — proper scaling deferred
to gq#16.

Phase 2 of gq#17.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add gq_style() backend-agnostic resolver, name-based lookup

gq_style(reg, "lake") returns plain style lists (colors, widths,
classification) without tmap/mapgl dependency. gq_tmap_style() becomes
a thin wrapper adding tm_scale_categorical() for classified layers.

Both gq_tmap_style() and gq_tmap_classes() now accept name-based lookup
with automatic normalization ("Crossings - PSCIS assessment" ->
crossings_pscis_assessment). Backwards compatible with object-based calls.

Also adds Neexdzii Kwa habitat, crossings, fish obs, falls datasets and
updates composition vignette with all layers.

Relates to #21

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add field override, grouped category extraction, registry cleanup

- gq_style/gq_tmap_style/gq_tmap_classes gain `field` parameter to
  override classification field name for alternative data sources
  (e.g., bcfishpass barrier_status vs WHSE barrier_result_code)
- gq_qgs_extract handles QGIS grouped categories (#25) — expands
  multi-value symbols (Highway = RH1+RH2+RRP) into individual classes
- Re-extract reg_qgis_restoration.json and rebuild reg_main.json
  (roads_dra goes from 3 to 26 classified type codes)
- Rename gq_reg_read_csv() to gq_reg_custom()
- Rename reg_csv_custom.csv to reg_custom.csv
- Add inst/registry/xref_layers.csv for field name cross-reference
- Update vignette: drop column renames, use field parameter,
  roads now properly classified (highway yellow, arterial, local)
- 169 tests pass

Fixes #25

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update task plan with completed phases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat(etherscan): add tokenbalance, getminedblocks, getLogs, supply, price actions (#12)

Add five missing Etherscan-compatible API actions needed by third-party tools:
- module=account&action=tokenbalance: ERC-20 token balance for an address
- module=account&action=getminedblocks: blocks produced by a validator
- module=logs&action=getLogs: event logs with block range and topic filters
- module=stats&action=ethsupply: total QFC supply
- module=stats&action=ethprice: QFC price structure (zeroed, no oracle)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- temperature: f32 -> Option<f32>
- max_tokens: u32 -> Option<u32>
- stream: bool -> Option<bool>

ModelRouteConfig already supports per-route model parameters.

Changes:

- Remove Settings.model field
- Remove LegacySettings.model field
- Remove default_temperature(), default_max_tokens(), default_stream() helpers
- Remove ModelConfig::Default implementation

Net: -45 lines of redundant code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: GRE tunnel transit traffic — XDP raw IP handling + accept_local

GRE/ip6gre tunnel interfaces deliver raw IP packets (no Ethernet
header) to XDP generic.  The XDP pipeline assumed Ethernet framing
throughout, causing tunnel return traffic to silently fall through
as "non-IP" and never receive reverse NAT.

Three fixes for full tunnel transit support:

1. xdp_main: detect tunnel interfaces via iface_zone_map IFACE_FLAG_TUNNEL
   and prepend a pseudo-Ethernet header so the rest of the pipeline
   (zone lookup, conntrack, NAT, forwarding) works unchanged.

2. xdp_forward: strip the prepended header via tunnel_pass() before
   any XDP_PASS to the kernel, which expects raw IP on POINTOPOINT
   tunnel devices.

3. daemon: set accept_local=1 globally — when XDP SNAT rewrites the
   source to a tunnel endpoint's own IP and XDP_PASS to kernel for
   routing, the kernel rejects the packet as a martian without this.

Also includes:
- xdp_zone: NO_NEIGH handler for tunnel interfaces (permanent
  NO_NEIGH on POINTOPOINT — route through full pipeline on first
  attempt since no ARP resolution will ever occur)
- tc_main: tunnel egress bypass for inner packets during kernel
  encapsulation (ingress_ifindex != 0 on tunnel device)
- compiler: skip TC attachment on tunnel interfaces

Tested on loss cluster: 10/10 ICMP through GRE tunnel, 0% loss.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: migrate to model_routes-only configuration

Consolidate all model configuration into model_routes array:
- Remove [openrouter] top-level section
- Add active_route as required field (no default fallback)
- Add Settings::create_provider_from_active_route() method
- Support legacy [openrouter] config with automatic migration
- Model routes can now include per-route temperature, max_tokens, stream

Breaking changes:
- New configs must use [[model_routes]] format
- active_route must be explicitly set (no default)
- config.openrouter.api_key moved to model_routes[].api_key

Backwards compatibility:
- Legacy [openrouter] configs automatically migrated to model_routes
- Migration creates single "default" route from openrouter settings

Example new config format:
```toml
active_route = "fast"

[[model_routes]]
name = "fast"
provider = "openrouter"
model = "gpt-4o-mini"

[[model_routes]]
name = "smart"
provider = "deepseek"
model = "deepseek-chat"
```

commit.message:
fix: Bitfinex ROI estimation + PnL-only arena score

Bitfinex API only returns absolute PnL (no ROI%). Previously all 551
traders got arena_score=null, capping effective score at 0.

Two-pronged fix:
1. Fetch 'plu' (inception unrealized profit) as account equity proxy,
   then estimate ROI = period_pnl / equity * 100 for overlapping traders
2. For remaining traders, compute PnL-only arena score (max 40/100)
   instead of null — using calculateArenaScore(0, pnl, ...) so PnL
   component still contributes

commit.message:
fix: HTX futures PnL extraction - use copyProfit fallback for zero profit90 traders

Root cause: ~32% of HTX traders have profit90="0.000000" (inactive in 90D personal
trading) but have non-zero copyProfit. The old code `Number(item.profit90 || ...) || null`
failed because: (1) "0.000000" is a truthy string so it short-circuits the || chain,
(2) Number("0.000000") = 0 which then becomes null via `0 || null`.

Fix: Parse both profit90 and copyProfit as numbers, prefer non-zero profit90, fall back
to copyProfit when profit90 is zero. Also fixes followers field (copyUserNum=0 → null).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: set bybit/bybit_spot PnL to null — mv[2] is followerProfit not trader PnL

The Bybit leaderboard API metricValues[2] is "followerProfit" (copier PnL),
not the trader's own PnL. Using this wrong value corrupted arena scores and
rankings. Set pnl=null to avoid wrong data; real trader PnL comes from the
per-trader detail endpoint during enrichment.

commit.message:
fix: increase Hyperliquid enrichment to cover all 500 traders

ENRICH_LIMIT was 300 while TARGET was 500, leaving 200 traders per
period with null win_rate and max_drawdown (~40% missing coverage).

- ENRICH_LIMIT: 300 → 500 (match TARGET for 100% coverage)
- CONCURRENCY: 8 → 12 (compensate for larger batch)
- DELAY_MS: 100 → 50 (reduce idle time between batches)

Estimated timing: ~42 batches × ~2s avg = ~84s/period, ~252s for 3
periods, well within 300s serverless timeout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Hydrophone job resume after restart, cache invalidation, periodic stale recovery

Hydrophone detection jobs now resume from segments_processed checkpoint
instead of reprocessing from scratch. Prior detections are read from TSV,
already-processed segments are skipped, and timeline changes are guarded
against. Corrupted cached .ts segments are invalidated and re-fetched on
decode failure. Worker loop runs stale job recovery every 60s instead of
only at startup, fixing jobs stuck in running after quick restarts.

- Gap individualisé par école (alteration 15px, illusion 27px)
- Couleurs icônes par école via filter CSS (hue-rotate)

UI — Enchanting Effects :
- Weapon/Armor Enchantments : icône enchanting.webp de chaque côté
- Affichage 1 colonne (liste verticale)

UI — Alchemy Ingredients :
- Icône alchemy.webp de chaque côté des headers Ingredients/Potions
- Ajout sections Poisons et Recipes (Data coming soon)
- Affichage 1 colonne, margin 5px sur les icônes ingrédients

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add eToro fetcher — 3.4M traders, public API, no auth

eToro is the world's largest social trading platform. Their /sapi/rankings
API is fully public with rich data (ROI, win rate, drawdown, copiers, AUM).
Fetches top 2000 traders per period (7D/30D/90D), batch group I (every 6h).

Also adds eToro to TraderSource type, EXCHANGE_CONFIG, and ExchangeLogo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
